backend/main.mo: Main backend actor implementing video clip management, user profiles, trending analytics, and AI clip suggestions with role-based access control (admin/user permissions) using Map-based storage for clips and profiles.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting three high-confidence features related to debugging admin panel access errors, fixing AdminPanel rendering issues, and verifying admin route protection logic.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Comprehensive summaries of all frontend files including components, hooks, and configuration, describing the React application structure with TanStack Router, authentication, admin panel, video clip management, and AI suggestions.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom OKLCH color system defining CSS variables for light and dark themes, including primary, secondary, destructive, and sidebar color tokens.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React application with title "Beast Clipping" and a root div mounting point.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React application component with TanStack Router setup, managing home page state for video playback and clip selection, and admin route protection with authentication checks, error boundaries, and detailed console logging for debugging.
frontend/src/components/AdminErrorBounda: React error boundary component that catches and displays errors in the admin panel with detailed error messages, stack traces, component stacks, and recovery options.
frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx: Admin dashboard component displaying user identity, total clips count, trending analytics, and a table of top clips with viral scores. Includes loading states, error handling with retry, and principal ID copy functionality.
frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx: Card component for displaying individual video clips with thumbnail, title, duration, viral score badge, and delete functionality. Includes confirmation dialog for deletion and visual indication when selected.
frontend/src/components/ClipList.tsx: Displays a scrollable list of saved video clips using ClipCard components. Shows loading state, empty state, and clip count with selection and deletion handlers.
Shows either AI-generated clip suggestions or trending viral clips with viral scores, timestamps, and action buttons to use suggested clips. Supports scrollable list with formatted time displays.
frontend/src/components/ClipTimestampCon: Form component for creating clips with title input and start/end timestamp controls (minutes/seconds). Handles clip creation, validation, and updates from selected clips or suggestions.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Main layout wrapper with header containing app branding, authentication button (login/logout), and admin panel navigation link for authenticated users. Includes query cache clearing on logout.
frontend/src/components/VideoUrlForm.tsx: Form component for entering YouTube URLs with validation, video loading, and AI clip generation functionality. Displays validation errors and loading states for both video loading and AI clip generation.
Badge component that displays a viral potential score (0-100) with color-coded styling and icons based on score thresholds (high/medium/low). Supports different sizes and optional label display.
frontend/src/components/YouTubePlayer.ts: Simple YouTube video player component that embeds a YouTube iframe with responsive 16:9 aspect ratio using a videoId prop.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: React Query hook that manages the backend actor instance, creating authenticated or anonymous actors based on Internet Identity login state. Invalidates dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminStats.ts: Hook for fetching admin statistics including total clips count and trending clip analytics from the backend actor. Uses React Query for caching and error handling.
Hook for AI-powered clip generation that currently uses mock data to suggest viral clip segments with titles, timestamps, and viral scores. Includes mutation handling and query invalidation on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipCreation.ts: Hook for creating video clips with automatic viral score calculation based on duration and title characteristics. Handles mutation and invalidates related queries on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useClips.ts: Hook for fetching all clips and deleting individual clips via the backend actor. Manages loading states and tracks which clip is currently being deleted.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipTimestamps.ts: Hook for managing clip start/end timestamps with separate minutes and seconds inputs, validation logic, and utilities for converting between formats and calculating durations.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Context provider and hook for Internet Identity authentication, managing login/logout flow, identity state, and AuthClient lifecycle. Handles identity persistence and expiration with configurable options.
frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts: React hook that checks if the current user is an admin by calling the backend's isCallerAdmin() method, with extensive logging for debugging authentication and authorization flow.
frontend/src/hooks/useTrendingClips.ts: React Query hook that fetches trending video clips from the backend actor when available.
Custom hook providing YouTube URL validation and video ID extraction utilities using regex patterns.
frontend/src/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for light and dark theme color schemes using OKLCH color space.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that sets up React Query and Internet Identity providers, then renders the root App component.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for extracting, persisting, and managing URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage integration.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining custom color tokens using OKLCH, container settings, animations, and plugin integrations for typography and container queries.
project_state.json: Project metadata tracking architectural decisions, known issues (admin panel authentication problems), and completed features for a YouTube video clipping application.
